title: $logical
text: $logical Return the value of an application logical. $logical ( LogicalName ) Example: vDir = $logical("export_dir") Parameters Table 1. Parameters Parameter Data Type Description LogicalName String Name of a logical symbol defined in the [LOGICALS] section of the assignment file. Return Values Value associated with the logical symbol name. Empty string ( "" ), if LogicalName is not found. Use Allowed in all component types. Example: Setting Directory Locations in ProcScript You can use logicals to allow you to spawn platform-specific commands. Consider the following section in an assignment file: [LOGICALS] work_directory = c:/my_app/test/work Copy This logical symbol can be used in ProcScript to refer to the appropriate directory: WorkDir = $logical("work_directory") SubDir = "%%$logical("work_directory")%%%/axel" Copy The results of these statements are: WorkDir contains c:/my_app/test/work SubDir contains c:/my_app/test/work/axel Example: Enabling the Debugger in a Deployed Application To enable the debugger to be started in a deployed application: Add the following ProcScript to the keyboardSwitch trigger of the application shell: trigger keyboardSwitch if ($logical("SwitchKeyboard") = "debug") debug endif end Copy If debugging is required for some reason in the deployed application, add SwitchKeyboard=debug to the [LOGICALS] section of the assignment file, and activate the trigger by pressing GOLD Y. Related Links [LOGICALS] Use the [LOGICALS] section of the assignment file to assign values to logicals that can be accessed using the $logical ProcScript function. Each application can have its own set of logicals. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
